case_title: CITY OF ARCATA ET AL VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY?S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE SUBMITTED WITH ITS REPLY BRIEF (TRANSACTION ID # 66079746) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY OF ARCATA CITY OF BLUE LAKE CITY OF CUPERTINO CITY OF DINUBA CITY OF MONTEREY CITY OF ORANGE COVE CITY OF OROVILLE CITY OF SACRAMENTO CITY OF SALINAS CITY OF SAN LEANDRO CITY OF SANGER CITY OF SANTA CRUZ CITY OF SUNNYVALE CITY OF VALLEJO COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY THE ACTION, PENDING THE RESOLUTION OF THE PENDING APPEAL IN CITY OF TORRANCE V. SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA EDISON CO., NO. B300296 (2D APP. DIST.) IS GRANTED. THE APPEAL IS FULLY BRIEFED, AND THE SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT COURT OF APPEAL HAS STATED THAT IT EXPECTS TO HEAR THE MATTER BEFORE THE END OF THE YEAR. PLAINTIFFS ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE ORDINANCES AT ISSUE IN THE TWO ACTIONS ARE "SUBSTANTIALLY SIMILAR," AS ARE AT LEAST SOME OF THE ISSUES POSED. FOR THE REASONS WELL STATED BY THE SANTA BARBARA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT IN STAYING THE CITY OF ARCADIA ACTION, A BRIEF STAY PENDING THE RESOLUTION OF THE APPEAL WOULD FURTHER THE INTERESTS OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND EFFICIENCY AND WOULD NOT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED.

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEC-02-2022 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFFS CITY OF ARCATA, CITY OF BLUE LAKE, CITY OF CUPERTINO, CITY OF DINUBA, CITY OF MONTEREY, CITY OF ORANGE COVE, CITY OF OROVILLE, CITY OF PACIFIC GROVE, CITY OF SACRAMENTO, CITY OF SALINAS, CITY OF SAN LEANDRO, CITY OF SANGER, CITY OF SANTA CRUZ, CITY OF SUNNYVALE, CITY OF VALLEJO, AND COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO'S MOTION FOR PENALITIES AND INTEREST. THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: RAQUEL SHARP, CSR NO. 10619, EMAIL: RAQUEL@TRANSCRIPTLEGAL.COM, TEL: 925-384-0003 (302/CK) ***** AFTER THE HEARING, THE COURT SIGNS AN ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF CITIES' MOTION FOR PENALTIES AND INTEREST. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) (302/CK) *****

Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Companys Notice Of Motion To Stay The Action
Off Calendar Nov-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Stay The Action, pending the resolution of the pending appeal in City of Torrance v. Southern California Edison Co., No. B300296 (2d App. Dist.) is GRANTED. The appeal is fully briefed, and the Second Appellate District Court of Appeal has stated that it expects to hear the matter before the end of the year. Plaintiffs acknowledge that the ordinances at issue in the two actions are "substantially similar," as are at least some of the issues posed. For the reasons well stated by the Santa Barbara County Superior Court in staying the City of Arcadia action, a brief stay pending the resolution of the appeal would further the interests of judicial economy and efficiency and would not prejudice plaintiffs. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Proceedings not reported.
